headline: Moderna (MRNA) Is Down 5.5% After FDA Sets Flu Vaccine Review, Tightens Lawsuit Venue Rules - What's Changed
summary: On May 6, 2026, Moderna’s board amended its bylaws to require Securities Act of 1933 lawsuits be filed exclusively in US federal district courts, aligning with recent Delaware law changes, while the FDA set a June 18 advisory panel review for the company’s mRNA-based seasonal flu vaccine after an earlier filing setback. Together with ongoing insider share sales under a preset trading plan and fresh real-world data on its pediatric COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness, these developments sharpen...
